title: GWO Basic Safety Training (BST)
description: The Global Wind Organisation Basic Safety Training is the industry-standard safety certification required for all personnel working on wind turbines. Covers first aid, manual handling, fire awareness, working at height, and sea survival.
entryRequirements: No prior qualifications required. Must be medically fit for offshore work.

title: Certificate in Offshore Wind Turbine Maintenance
description: Comprehensive programme covering the maintenance, diagnosis, and repair of modern offshore wind turbines. Includes hands-on training with turbine simulators and real-world case studies from Irish offshore projects.
entryRequirements: Level 6 qualification in Electrical/Mechanical Engineering or equivalent trade qualification.

title: Electrical Systems for Offshore Wind Energy
description: Covers high-voltage electrical systems specific to offshore wind including subsea cables, offshore substations, grid connection, and power conversion systems.
entryRequirements: BEng in Electrical Engineering or equivalent.

title: Marine Coordination for Offshore Operations
description: Training programme for marine coordinators working on offshore wind installation and maintenance campaigns. Covers vessel management, weather routing, logistics planning, and emergency procedures.

title: Environmental Impact Assessment for Offshore Wind
description: Specialist module covering the EIA process for offshore wind developments in Irish waters. Includes marine ecology, consenting procedures, stakeholder engagement, and cumulative impact assessment.

title: SCADA Data Analysis for Wind Energy
description: Learn to analyse SCADA data from wind turbines to identify performance issues, predict failures, and optimise energy output. Hands-on with Python, Power BI, and real anonymised datasets.

title: Project Management for Renewable Energy
description: CPD programme covering project management methodologies applied to renewable energy projects. Includes risk management, stakeholder engagement, and regulatory navigation specific to Irish energy projects.

title: Composite Repair for Wind Turbine Blades
description: Practical training in composite material repair techniques for wind turbine blades. Covers damage assessment, surface preparation, resin systems, and quality control.

title: IRATA Rope Access Level 1
description: Entry-level rope access qualification enabling work at height on wind turbines, offshore structures, and industrial installations.

title: Introduction to Offshore Wind Energy
description: A foundational overview of the offshore wind energy sector in Ireland and globally. Covers technology, policy landscape, career opportunities, supply chain, and Ireland's pipeline of offshore projects.

title: HSE Management for Offshore Wind Operations
description: Covers health, safety, and environmental management systems specific to offshore wind. Includes risk assessment methodologies, incident investigation, regulatory compliance, and safety culture development.

title: Commercial Management in the Energy Sector
description: Executive programme covering contract management, procurement strategy, financial modelling, and stakeholder management for energy sector professionals transitioning into commercial leadership roles.

title: Supply Chain & Logistics for Offshore Wind
description: Specialist programme covering port operations, vessel scheduling, heavy lift logistics, and supply chain optimisation for offshore wind construction and O&M campaigns.

title: Micro-Credential: Wind Energy Fundamentals
description: Stackable micro-credential covering wind resource assessment, turbine technology, grid integration, and energy storage. Part of the university's new renewable energy pathway.

title: BOSIET — Basic Offshore Safety Induction & Emergency Training
description: Mandatory offshore survival training covering helicopter underwater escape, sea survival, firefighting, and first aid. Required before any offshore deployment.
